---
title: "Process many samples (param_list)"
description: "Per-sample arguments plus shared settings in one run."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 25
---

[`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>

A **`param_list`** processes a whole cohort in a single command. It is a list
where each entry is one sample carrying that sample's own arguments; any argument
set outside the list applies to every sample. See the
[param_list concept](../concepts/param-list.qmd) for the full model.

## Event-specific and global parameters

In the list below, a multi-event Nextflow channel is created for two inputs.
Each carries an `id` and any per-sample (event-specific) parameters:

```yaml
# param_list.yaml
- id: foo
  input: foo.h5mu
  rna_min_counts: 2      # only for foo
- id: bar
  input: bar.h5mu
  rna_min_counts: 1      # only for bar
```

Combine event-specific parameters with **global** parameters that apply to every
event:

```bash
nextflow run ... --param_list param_list.yaml --rna_max_counts 1000000
```

Event-specific parameters always override globals of the same name.

## Supported file types

The `param_list` can be a YAML, JSON, or CSV file.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## CSV

```
id,input,rna_min_counts
foo,foo.h5mu,2
bar,bar.h5mu,1
```

## JSON

```json
[
  { "id": "foo", "input": "foo.h5mu", "rna_min_counts": 2 },
  { "id": "bar", "input": "bar.h5mu", "rna_min_counts": 1 }
]
```

## YAML

```yaml
- id: foo
  input: foo.h5mu
  rna_min_counts: 2
- id: bar
  input: bar.h5mu
  rna_min_counts: 1
```

:::

## Run a cohort through process_samples

`process_samples` is the recommended entry point for a full cohort — it reads the
`param_list`, runs per-sample QC and multisample normalization, and emits one
processed object:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/multiomics/process_samples/main.nf \
  --param_list samples.csv \
  --publish_dir results/
```

For very large cohorts, `process_batches` runs the multisample steps on groups
of samples in parallel before merging, reducing peak memory use.

## Passing the list through a params-file

Both event-specific and global parameters can live in one Nextflow
`-params-file`:

```yaml
# params-file.yaml
param_list:
  - id: foo
    input: foo.h5mu
  - id: bar
    input: bar.h5mu
rna_max_counts: 1000000
```

```bash
nextflow run ... -params-file params-file.yaml
```

## Resolving paths

Files in a `param_list` are resolved relative to the location of the list file
itself — including when the list lives on a remote location such as an S3 bucket.
Absolute paths are used as-is.

**Next:** [Process & filter samples →](process-filter.qmd) ·
[param_list concept →](../concepts/param-list.qmd)
